quiz_title: CMFAS Module 9A - Mock Exam Set B

questions.question: Most derivatives/structured products come with an expiry date, which instrument below does not have an expiry date?

David seeks returns that are higher than those from traditional fixed income products. He is willing to accept slightly higher risk for the higher returns. Which of the following should he invest in ?
The primary risk to the principal component is ____________?
For discount certificates, at maturity the investor receives either a cash settlement or the underlying stock. Under which scenario below would the investor receive a cash settlement?
options: Price of the underlying stock is equal to capped strike
Price of the underlying stock is below the capped strike
Price of the underlying stock is above the capped strike
questions.correct_answer: Price of the underlying stock is above the capped strike

questions.question: The difference between the spot price and the exercise price is known as_________?
Only those ETFs that use derivatives are Structured Funds, they are also known as _______?
When explaining a structured product to clients, disclosure must be in plain language, avoid technical terms & presented in a format that is easy to understand. This is found under the ____________.

Laureen believes she can make money from the stock market, especially when volatility is high. She wants to be able to make money whether the stock goes up or goes down by a large amount. Which of the following would be the appropriate strategy for her?

Ronald is keen to start trading options on stocks after taking a course. He opened an account with an options broker to execute and clear his positions. The broker informed Ronald that there is one strategy that he is not permitted to execute/enter as it is risky and losses may be unlimited. What strategy is this?

Donald wants to buy XYZ shares but feels that the current market price of $32 is too expensive. He wants to wait a month if necessary before deciding. What can Donald do such that he is able to still buy XYZ shares at a discount to the current market price, whether XYZ shares rises or falls?
options: Enter into a covered call position

questions.question: Rebecca runs a health café selling fresh fruit juices. Since her business is doing quite well, Rebecca has entered into a contract with a fruit wholesaler to delivery fresh fruits every 2 weeks. On one such delivery Rebecca found that all the fruits delivered were either spoilt or rotten, and as a result she had to close her café for a week, which resulted in operating losses. What sort of risk did Rebecca suffer?
An investor places S$50,000 in an AUD investment where SGD$0.90 = AUD$1.00. The investment subsequently depreciated by 6% but AUD strengthen where AUD$1 = SGD$1.10. Calculate the gain or loss in SGD.
Which of the following fees & charges are applicable to Portfolio Bonds?
Portfolio bonds can be used as a source to provide regular income, how is this achieved?
options: It is derived from the coupon pay-out from investing in bonds
It is derived from dividend pay-out from stocks owned
It is obtained both from coupon & dividend pay-outs from both bonds & stocks
It is obtained from a redemption of units, i.e. sale of investments
questions.correct_answer: It is obtained from a redemption of units, i.e. sale of investments

questions.question: Jenny invested in an interest rate futures and needs to deposit S$4000 as initial margin with her broker. The maintenance margin for the futures contract is S$3500. The value of her contract drops by S$1,000. How much is her margin call?

A structured ILP invests in AAA Fund which has an initial sales charge of 5% and an annual management fee of 2%. The net return of the fund is shown below:

After 1 year:
Gross return: 2.00% Net return: -1.20% 

After 2 years:
Gross return: 1.30% Net return: -0.50% 

After 3 years:
Gross return: 2.50% Net return: -0.20% 

What can you deduce from the information above?
options: The fund manager has performed below par
The initial sales charge is irrelevant in determining net fund performance
Charges to the fund are necessary to ensure the fund delivers good results
The initial sales charge still drags down the performance of the fund after 3 years
questions.correct_answer: The initial sales charge still drags down the performance of the fund after 3 years

questions.question: Hui Fong has invested in a structured fund called Better Fund. The unit was sold at $1 at inception, and after 2 years its net asset value is $1.10. What does the change in net asset reflect?
options: Better fund has sustained a loss of 10%
Better fund has enjoyed a gain of 10%
The nominal value is lower than the real value by 10%
questions.correct_answer: Better fund has enjoyed a gain of 10%

questions.question: Redemption is ___________.
options: The purchase of units in ILP sub-funds
The sale of units in ILP sub-funds
The return of units in ILP sub-funds
The spread between the bid & offer price
questions.correct_answer: The return of units in ILP sub-funds

questions.question: Lori is keen to subscribe to a corporate bond issue by First Capital that is AAA rated. However the minimum subscription amount is $250,000, Lori has on hand only $50,000. What can Lori do to still be able to subscribe to First Capital bond issue?
options: Lori can ask her parents to subscribe on her behalf
Lori can use leverage via a Futures contract
Lori can subscribe via a convertible bond instead
Lori can subscribe via a Collective Investment Scheme
questions.correct_answer: Lori can subscribe via a Collective Investment Scheme

questions.question: If the spot price is $1300, and the cost of carry is a premium of $100, calculate the forward price.

Edward is an experienced futures trader and trades various regional stock indexes. He decides to sell the Hang Seng Index (HSI) at 19000 points. The HSI has a multiplier of HKD$50 per index point. A week later the Hang Seng Index rises to 19100 points, & Edward decides to close his position. Calculate his gain/loss on his futures position.
Options that permit the holder or writer to extend the maturity date by a specified period of time are known as__________?

Annie purchased a Structured ILP, however 10 days later she changed her mind and decides to cancel it. She was already informed earlier that should she cancel before 14 days, she will receive a full refund of the premium paid adjusted for any downward change in the net asset value of the ILP during that period. What risk does Annie face?
The value of an ILP sub-fund is based on the NAV of the sub-fund. Which of the following fund is an exception to this?
What information must be disclosed in the Product Summary during the sales process?
Issuers of callable bonds have the right to "call" or redeem their bonds before the maturity dates. Under which scenario will they likely do so?
If a commodity trader has an expected basis of -US$0.40, and the current market is offering a basis of -US$0.80, this is known as a __________.

Strengthen Insurance Pte Ltd has introduced an Investment-linked Policy that invests into a variety of funds, including a Structured Fund called MERCY Fund.

The table below is an extract from the prospectus of MERCY Fund:
Past Performance of the Sub-Fund and Benchmark
Annualized Returns (Average Annual Compounded Returns)

A client approached his financial advisor for advice. Based on the information above, which of the following statements made by the financial advisor is TRUE? Based on the fund's average annual compounded returns the MERCY Fund:
options: Had out-performed the benchmark since inception
Had out-performed the benchmark for the past 5 years
Had under-performed the benchmark for the past 10 years
Can almost guarantee investors an annualized return of at least 1%
questions.correct_answer: Had out-performed the benchmark for the past 5 years

questions.question:
Jim decided to invest $10,000 into a Structured ILP, he was attracted to it as the investment component was channelled into a structured bond, that offered higher coupon pay-out than traditional bonds. The stated coupon pay-out over the investment period of 5 years is 15% and 100% capital return at maturity. At maturity Jim got back total coupon pay-out of 12% & 90% of his principal. His advisor mention that the
underlying assets did not perform to expectation due to challenging market conditions.

Calculate Jim's return.
What is the main difference between a Portfolio Bond and a Unit Trust?
For bonus certificates, if the price of the underlying asset does not fall below the barrier level, at maturity the investor receives a return no lower than the ____________.
Which of the following is true for Collateral risk?
options: It is the same as counterparty risk
It is present whenever collateral is used as a guarantee in a loan agreement
questions.correct_answer: It is present whenever collateral is used as a guarantee in a loan agreement

questions.question: Gary is an Australian and has invested AUD$150,000 into a US Fund. The fund is AAA rated and consist of stocks & fixed income products. What risk should Gary be most concerned with?

If the manager of the ILP sub-fund believes that the transacted price is not representative or not available to the market, the NAV should then be based on "fair value" of the assets. What below correctly defines fair value?
options: Fair value is the value obtained by selling in the market just before it closes
Fair value is the valuation price decided by the market maker
Fair value is the price obtained after selling in the market and deducting commission & other charges
Fair Value is the price that the fund can reasonably expect to receive upon the current sale of the asset, determined with due care & in good faith.
questions.correct_answer: Fair Value is the price that the fund can reasonably expect to receive upon the current sale of the asset, determined with due care & in good faith.

questions.question: Which statement is true for Benefit Illustration with regards to Structured ILP?
options: Benefit Illustration should be furnished to investors of the Structured ILP at point-of-sale.
Two rates of returns should be included in the Benefit Illustration to demonstrate the certainty of potential return of the Structured ILP
MAS is responsible for issuing the guidelines on Benefit Illustration
questions.correct_answer: Benefit Illustration should be furnished to investors of the Structured ILP at point-of-sale.

questions.question: Investors are often attracted to structured products due to the high potential pay-outs, however a structured product delivers return to the investor only when_________?
options: Market view has to be correct
Strategy to capture the market view need not be appropriate
Pricing on the structure is expensive
questions.correct_answer: Market view has to be correct

questions.question: A Portfolio Bond is suitable for an investor with a __________ time frame?

In a policy statement that Rachel is reading, she notes an early redemption clause that says: 

"After the third month, if the portfolio of benchmark stocks is equal to or above 110% of their initial stock prices, the single premium will be returned, together with the pro-rata annual pay-out. The policy terminates after the payment is made."

What is the implication of this term for the policy owner?
options: When the condition is met, the policy owner faces counterparty risk.
When the condition is met, the policy owner enjoys the return from a rising stock market.
When the condition is met, the policy owner enjoys the privilege of getting back his money with no reinvestment risk.
When the condition is met, the policy owner receives the original single premium and prorated pay-out and faces the risk of reinvesting in a rising market.
questions.correct_answer: When the condition is met, the policy owner receives the original single premium and prorated pay-out and faces the risk of reinvesting in a rising market.

questions.question: George is a financial advisor, his friend Jim is interested in Structured ILPs, which of the following are possible reasons why Jim is not suitable?
options: He has a short term time frame
He has a low risk threshold
He expects some guarantee in his investment performance

questions.question: Drip feeding is fund switching implemented in small doses, which of the following is a likely reason for using drip feeding?
Which statement below is Incorrect about a Bonus certificate?
options: Bonus certificates are tracker certificates with limited downside protection
For bonus certificates once a "knock-out" occurs the downside protection no longer exists
Bonus certificates can only be "knocked-out" at maturity
Bonus certificates carry full upside potential with no downside protection once a knock-out occurs.
questions.correct_answer: Bonus certificates can only be "knocked-out" at maturity

questions.question: George is a coffee farmer who can only sell his beans a year later. He anticipates the prices to be weaker then compared to now, how can he protect himself?

Which statement/document would contain the following information?

i. number & value of units held at end of current statement period
ii. premium received during the statement period
iii. current death benefit at the end of the current statement period
iv. net cash surrender value at the end of the current statement period
The most common swap is an interest rate swap. All the following statements are correct about an interest rate swap except__________?
options: An interest rate swap is between 2 parties, one party prefers to borrow on a fixed rate loan, while the other party prefers to borrow on a floating rate loan.
An interest rate swap is between 2 parties, whereby both parties either borrow at a fixed rate or both parties borrow on a floating rate.
The purpose of entering into an interest rate swap, is for both parties to lower their borrowing cost
Calculations for interest rate swaps are based on a "notional" principal amount
questions.correct_answer: An interest rate swap is between 2 parties, whereby both parties either borrow at a fixed rate or both parties borrow on a floating rate.

questions.question: Jamie sold a Put option on XYZ shares. Her Put option has an exercise price of $25, and cost $1.50. Within the next 5 days, XYZ rose from $22 to $27. Calculate the percentage change in the intrinsic value of the option.

Cory's family has recently moved to Singapore, and has set up a Single Family Office (SFO). His father has instructed him to setup a fund comprising of South Asia blue-chip shares. His father also mention that the fund must be diversified to withstand possible challenging market conditions. Understanding this requirement, Cory would proceed to select stocks with a correlation of _______?

Lauren enjoys staying up late to watch & trade the US stock index futures market. She is short term bullish on the Nasdaq, and decides to go Long the E-mini Nasdaq-100 futures contract at 15600 points. The multiplier for this contract is US$20 per index point. Two days later the index rose to 15680 and Lauren decided to close her futures position. 

Calculate Lauren's gain/loss.

Richard is eagerly anticipating the release of the brand new iPhone 15 in September. He feels that this revolutionary release would be positive for Apple shares, and would like to take a position. He prefers to buy a call option on Apple shares instead of buying the stock. Which reason below would buying the stock be a better choice?
options: He has a long term time frame
He is looking for unlimited upside
He is looking for limited downside
questions.correct_answer: He has a long term time frame

questions.question: The cash price of Platinum is currently trading at US$980, while the September Futures contract for Platinum is trading at US$960. Joe your client rings you to check on the Platinum market, what is the correct way to describe the current market to Joe?
options: Platinum is currently experiencing a contango market
Platinum is currently experiencing a backwardation market
Platinum is showing a strong basis
Platinum is showing a weak basis
questions.correct_answer: Platinum is currently experiencing a backwardation market

questions.question: What is the main advantage of exchange-traded structured products over other structured products?
